questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: project / the / completed / was / time / on
options: The project was completed on time
Was the project completed on time
Completed was the project on time
The project was completed on time
On time the project was completed
questions.solution: The project was completed on time

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The furniture (A) / in this room (B) / are very (C) / expensive. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: digital / has / way / the / changed / communication / we
options: Communication digital has changed the way we
We communicate way the has changed digital technology
Digital technology has changed the way we communicate
The way we communicate has changed digital technology
Digital technology has changed the way we communicate
questions.solution: Digital technology has changed the way we communicate

questions.stem: Find the error: The company (A) / is in (B) / the red (C) / for three years. (D)
options: D - should be 'has been in the red for three years'
questions.solution: D - should be 'has been in the red for three years'

Research this study has significant implications for

questions.solution: This study has significant implications for research

Announced the company its expansion plans has

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: globalisation / economies / has / world / around / the / transformed
options: Around the world globalisation has transformed economies
Globalisation has transformed economies around the world
Globalisation has transformed economies around the world
Transformed economies globalisation has around the world
Economies around the world has globalisation transformed
questions.solution: Globalisation has transformed economies around the world

options: A - should be 'She lost not only'
questions.solution: A - should be 'She lost not only'

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a purely capacitive AC circuit, the current:
options: Is in phase with the voltage
questions.solution: An ideal capacitor makes current lead voltage by 90°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about skin effect is correct?
options: Effective AC resistance of a conductor increases with frequency.
It makes current flow at the conductor centre.
It reduces resistance at high frequency.
questions.solution: Skin effect crowds AC current toward the surface, reducing the effective area and raising AC resistance with frequency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the divider shown (24 V applied), the output voltage Vout across R2 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The form factor of a sinusoidal waveform is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 100 V source drives a 12 Ω resistor. The current is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Power factor is defined as the cosine of the angle between:
questions.solution: Power factor = cos φ, where φ is the phase angle between voltage and current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Introducing a small air gap into an iron-cored magnetic circuit, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: The flux decreases for the same MMF.
The inductance of a coil on it decreases.
questions.solution: An air gap adds large reluctance (low permeability), so total reluctance rises and flux/inductance fall.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sinusoidal voltage shown has a peak of 311 V (marked). Its RMS value is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 120" width="300" height="120"><line x1="10" y1="60" x2="290" y2="60" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="10" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 60 Q 55 0 90 60 T 160 60 T 230 60 T 300 60" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="55" y1="60" x2="55" y2="10" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="60" y="14" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vm=311V</text><text x="270" y="74" font-size="10" fill="#475569">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sinusoidal voltage waveform

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An auto-transformer is more economical than a two-winding transformer mainly because:
options: Part of the power is transferred conductively, saving copper
questions.solution: In an auto-transformer only part of the power is transformed inductively; the conductive path reduces copper and size, best for ratios near 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The current-versus-frequency curve of a series RLC circuit peaks at the marked frequency f₀ ≈ 100 Hz. At this resonant frequency the power factor is:
questions.solution: At series resonance XL = XC, the impedance is purely resistive and current peaks, so the power factor is unity. (Here f₀ ≈ 100 Hz.)

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" height="130"><line x1="20" y1="110" x2="290" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="15" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 108 C 110 105 140 20 160 20 C 180 20 210 105 290 108" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="160" y1="20" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="160" y="124" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">f₀</text><text x="26" y="20" font-size="10" fill="#475569">I</text><text x="278" y="124" font-size="10" fill="#475569">f</text></svg>
figure.caption: Series-resonance current curve

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most widely used semiconductor material for power devices is:
questions.solution: Silicon dominates because of its stable oxide, temperature tolerance and cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Total power in a balanced 3-phase load is:
questions.solution: P = √3·VL·IL·cos φ in terms of line quantities.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The current-versus-frequency curve of a series RLC circuit peaks at the marked frequency f₀ ≈ 50 Hz. At this resonant frequency the power factor is:
questions.solution: At series resonance XL = XC, the impedance is purely resistive and current peaks, so the power factor is unity. (Here f₀ ≈ 50 Hz.)

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" height="130"><line x1="20" y1="110" x2="290" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="15" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 108 C 110 105 140 20 160 20 C 180 20 210 105 290 108" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="160" y1="20" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="160" y="124" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">f₀</text><text x="26" y="20" font-size="10" fill="#475569">I</text><text x="278" y="124" font-size="10" fill="#475569">f</text></svg>
figure.caption: Series-resonance current curve

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electrical power dissipated in a resistor can be written as:
questions.solution: P = VI = I²R = V²/R for a resistor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The impedance triangle shown has R = 6 Ω and X = 4 Ω. The impedance phase angle is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 140" width="200" height="140"><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="180" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="20" y2="10" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="120" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="90" y="134" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=6Ω</text><line x1="140" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="145" y="85" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">X=4Ω</text><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="70" y="78" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Z</text></svg>
figure.caption: Impedance triangle (R–X)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a balanced 3-phase star system, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: Line voltage leads its nearest phase voltage by 30°.
The line current is √3 times the phase current.
The line voltage is √3 times the phase voltage.
Line and phase currents are equal.
questions.solution: In star, line and phase currents are equal; the √3 factor applies to voltages, not currents.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tellegen's theorem is based fundamentally on:
questions.solution: Tellegen's theorem holds for any lumped network satisfying KVL/KCL, regardless of element nature.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A sinusoidal voltage has a peak value of 200 V. Its RMS value is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 4 Ω and 6 Ω in series give a total resistance of:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.1 H, C = 40 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.1×40µF)) ≈ 79.6 Hz.

figure.caption: Series RLC circuit

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a single line-to-ground fault, the three sequence networks are connected in:
questions.solution: An LG fault connects positive, negative and zero sequence networks in series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The main purpose of earthing the metal body of an appliance is:
options: Safety of personnel against electric shock
questions.solution: Earthing gives fault current a low-resistance path and keeps exposed metal near earth potential, protecting users.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has R = 10 Ω, XL = 20 Ω, XC = 12 Ω across 100 V. The current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: When the field flux of a separately excited DC motor is weakened (armature voltage constant), the speed:
questions.solution: Since N ∝ (V − IaRa)/φ, reducing flux φ raises the speed (the basis of field-weakening control).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer delivers 10 kW with iron loss 0.3 kW and copper loss 0.6 kW. Its efficiency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At series resonance, the impedance of an RLC circuit is:
questions.solution: XL and XC cancel, leaving only R, so impedance is minimum and resistive (current maximum).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Comparing an ideal and a real silicon diode in reverse bias, which is correct?
options: The ideal diode carries more reverse current.
The real diode carries a small reverse leakage current while the ideal one carries none.
questions.solution: An ideal diode blocks perfectly in reverse; a real diode has a small temperature-dependent leakage (saturation) current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coil has resistance 3 Ω and reactance 4 Ω. Its impedance magnitude is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a balanced star connection, the line voltage is related to the phase voltage by:
questions.solution: In star, line voltage leads phase voltage and VL = √3·Vph; line and phase currents are equal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a divider, 24 V is applied across 6 Ω and 6 Ω in series. The voltage across the 6 Ω resistor is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The no-load current of a transformer is small and highly reactive because:
options: It mainly supplies the magnetizing reactive power, with little core loss component
It supplies the full load power
questions.solution: No-load current is the magnetizing current plus a small loss component, so it is small and lags by nearly 90°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Negative feedback in an amplifier generally:
options: Reduces gain but increases bandwidth and stability
questions.solution: Negative feedback trades gain for stability, lower distortion and wider bandwidth (gain–bandwidth product is roughly constant).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the divider shown (12 V applied), the output voltage Vout across R2 is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 150" width="220" height="150"><line x1="30" y1="20" x2="30" y2="50" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="50" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">R1=3Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="84" x2="30" y2="100" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="100" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R2=6Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="134" x2="30" y2="145" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="30" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="40" y="96" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vout</text><text x="35" y="16" font-size="10" fill="#475569">12V</text></svg>
figure.caption: Resistive voltage divider

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a reciprocal two-port described by ABCD parameters, the condition satisfied is:
questions.solution: Reciprocity of a passive two-port gives the determinant AD − BC = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has R = 8 Ω, XL = 20 Ω, XC = 12 Ω across 100 V. The current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Over one complete cycle, the average power absorbed by a pure inductor is:
questions.solution: An inductor stores and returns energy; net real power over a cycle is zero (it only exchanges reactive power).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Increasing the air gap in a magnetic circuit (other things equal):
questions.solution: The air gap adds large reluctance (low permeability), so for the same MMF the flux falls.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A source has Vth = 24 V and Rth = 4 Ω. The maximum power deliverable to a matched load is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The type of a control system is the number of:
options: Poles at the origin of the open-loop transfer function
questions.solution: System 'type' = number of integrators (s-factors) in the denominator at s = 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method the readings are W₁ = 1000 W and W₂ = 400 W. The total 3-phase power is:
questions.solution: Total power = W₁ + W₂ = 1000 + 400 = 1400 W (the difference relates to reactive power).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL) is a statement of the conservation of:
questions.solution: KCL says the algebraic sum of currents at a node is zero — charge is neither created nor destroyed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time constant of a series RC circuit is:
questions.solution: τ = RC; after one time constant the capacitor charges to ~63% of the final voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A DC motor on 240 V draws 15 A with armature resistance 0.8 Ω. The back EMF is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a balanced delta connection, the line current is related to the phase current by:
questions.solution: In delta, line and phase voltages are equal and IL = √3·Iph.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a load with R = 3 Ω and reactance X = 6 Ω, the power factor is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3-phase synchronous motor is:
questions.solution: It produces no average starting torque and needs auxiliary (e.g. damper-winding) starting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The skin effect in an AC conductor causes:
options: Current to crowd at the centre
Current to crowd toward the surface, raising effective resistance
questions.solution: At higher frequency current concentrates near the surface, increasing the effective AC resistance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A current of 2 A flows through a 12 Ω resistor. The power dissipated is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A long transmission line is operated on no load. Which is correct?
options: The receiving-end voltage falls to zero.
The receiving-end voltage rises above the sending-end voltage (Ferranti effect).
The sending-end voltage rises above the receiving end.
questions.solution: Charging current through the line inductance raises the open-end voltage above the sending end (Ferranti effect).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 12 Ω and 4 Ω are connected in parallel. The equivalent resistance is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time constant of a series RL circuit is:
questions.solution: τ = L/R; the current reaches ~63% of its final value in one time constant.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Ferranti effect on a long, lightly loaded transmission line means:
questions.solution: Line capacitance draws leading current that raises the receiving-end voltage above the sending end under light load.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 4-pole, 60 Hz induction motor runs at 1770 rpm. Its slip is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The RMS value of a sinusoidal voltage of peak value Vm is:
questions.solution: For a sine wave, RMS = Vm/√2 ≈ 0.707 Vm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The synchronous speed of a 8-pole machine on a 60 Hz supply is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL) is a statement of the conservation of:
questions.solution: The sum of voltage drops around a closed loop equals zero — energy supplied equals energy absorbed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If one wattmeter in the two-wattmeter method reads zero, the load power factor is:
questions.solution: W₂ = VLIL cos(30°+φ) = 0 when 30°+φ = 90°, i.e. φ = 60°, pf = cos60° = 0.5.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The inductive reactance of a 0.1 H inductor at 50 Hz is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The equivalent resistance of two equal resistors R in parallel is:
questions.solution: For two equal parallel resistors, Req = R·R/(R+R) = R/2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A balanced 3-phase load draws 15 A at 440 V line, pf 0.9. The total power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An over-excited synchronous motor draws a current that is:
questions.solution: Over-excitation makes it draw leading current — used for power-factor correction (synchronous condenser).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in an inductor carrying current I is:
questions.solution: Energy = ½LI², stored in the magnetic field of the coil.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The capacitive reactance of a 50 µF capacitor at 60 Hz is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A moving-iron instrument can measure:
questions.solution: Deflection depends on the square of current, so it works on both AC and DC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 100 Ω, Q = 50 Ω and S = 60 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 100×60/50 = 120 Ω.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 160" width="200" height="160"><polygon points="100,20 180,80 100,140 20,80" fill="none" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="55" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">P=100Ω</text><text x="140" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Q=50Ω</text><text x="55" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=?</text><text x="135" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">S=60Ω</text><circle cx="100" cy="80" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="100" y="84" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">G</text></svg>
figure.caption: Wheatstone bridge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Corona discharge on a transmission line increases with:
questions.solution: Corona worsens as surface gradient rises (higher voltage, thinner or rough conductors, bad weather).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a purely inductive AC circuit, the current:
options: Is in phase with the voltage
questions.solution: An ideal inductor makes current lag voltage by 90°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.5 H, C = 40 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.5×40µF)) ≈ 35.6 Hz.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" width="300" height="90"><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="40" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="40" y="37" width="34" height="16" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="57" y="32" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">R</text><path d="M74 45 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="98" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">L=0.5H</text><line x1="122" y1="45" x2="150" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="150" y1="35" x2="150" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="158" y1="35" x2="158" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="154" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">C=40µF</text><line x1="158" y1="45" x2="290" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="290" y1="45" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="10" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="75" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="150" cy="75" r="3" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="88" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">~ AC source</text></svg>
figure.caption: Series RLC circuit

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A wattmeter reads zero on a purely reactive (inductive or capacitive) load because:
options: The average of v·i over a cycle is zero
questions.solution: Pure reactance gives a 90° phase shift; instantaneous power averages to zero over a cycle, so real power is zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer with 200 primary and 1000 secondary turns is fed 400 V. The secondary voltage is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electrical power is transmitted at high voltage mainly to:
questions.solution: For a given power, higher voltage means lower current, so I²R losses fall.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The average value of a sinusoidal current over one complete cycle is:
questions.solution: Over a full cycle the positive and negative halves cancel, giving zero average.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series RLC circuit has R = 20 Ω, L = 0.1 H, C = 10 µF. Its quality factor Q is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The resonant frequency of a series RLC circuit is given by:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, giving f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coil has resistance 9 Ω and reactance 4 Ω. Its impedance magnitude is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The holding current of a thyristor is:
options: The current needed to latch it on
The minimum anode current to keep it conducting after the gate is removed
questions.solution: Holding current keeps an already-on SCR conducting; latching current (larger) is needed to turn it on.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The EMF of a single lead-acid cell is about:
questions.solution: A lead-acid cell gives ~2 V; a 12 V battery uses six cells in series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 20 Ω, Q = 200 Ω and S = 60 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 20×60/200 = 6 Ω.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 160" width="200" height="160"><polygon points="100,20 180,80 100,140 20,80" fill="none" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="55" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">P=20Ω</text><text x="140" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Q=200Ω</text><text x="55" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=?</text><text x="135" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">S=60Ω</text><circle cx="100" cy="80" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="100" y="84" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">G</text></svg>
figure.caption: Wheatstone bridge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a capacitor of capacitance C charged to voltage V is:
questions.solution: Energy = ½CV², stored in the electric field between the plates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series RLC circuit has R = 5 Ω, L = 0.1 H, C = 10 µF. Its quality factor Q is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The SI unit of luminous flux is the:
questions.solution: Luminous flux is measured in lumens; illuminance (lumen/m²) is the lux.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PMMC (permanent-magnet moving-coil) instrument can directly measure:
questions.solution: PMMC responds to average value; on AC the average is zero, so it reads DC directly (needs a rectifier for AC).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a 1000 µF capacitor charged to 50 V is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The 'maximum demand' charge in a two-part tariff is based on:
options: The peak kVA/kW the consumer draws
questions.solution: A two-part tariff has a fixed charge tied to maximum demand plus an energy charge per kWh.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 4 Ω and 12 Ω are connected in series. The equivalent resistance is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Power-factor improvement of an inductive load is usually done by connecting:
questions.solution: A shunt capacitor supplies leading reactive power, raising the lagging power factor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For maximum power transfer to a complex load ZL from a source of impedance Zth, ZL must equal:
questions.solution: With a reactive source, maximum power needs ZL = Zth* so the reactances cancel and resistances match.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sinusoidal voltage shown has a peak of 200 V (marked). Its RMS value is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 120" width="300" height="120"><line x1="10" y1="60" x2="290" y2="60" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="10" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 60 Q 55 0 90 60 T 160 60 T 230 60 T 300 60" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="55" y1="60" x2="55" y2="10" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="60" y="14" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vm=200V</text><text x="270" y="74" font-size="10" fill="#475569">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sinusoidal voltage waveform

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Eddy-current loss in a magnetic core varies with frequency f and flux density Bm as:
questions.solution: Eddy loss ∝ f²Bm²t² (lamination thickness t); hysteresis loss ∝ f·Bm^1.6 (Steinmetz).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A copper wire (ρ = 1.7×10⁻⁸ Ω·m) is 100 m long with 1 mm² cross-section. Its resistance is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ohm's law states that, at constant temperature, the current through a conductor is:
options: Proportional to the square of the voltage
Directly proportional to the voltage across it
questions.solution: V = IR, so for constant R the current rises in direct proportion to the applied voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A diode conducts when it is:
questions.solution: A diode conducts in forward bias and blocks in reverse bias (ideally).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has L = 0.5 H and C = 10 µF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A current transformer (CT) secondary should never be:
questions.solution: An open CT secondary produces a dangerously high voltage and core saturation; it must be shorted if the meter is removed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The impedance triangle shown has R = 3 Ω and X = 4 Ω. The impedance phase angle is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 140" width="200" height="140"><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="180" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="20" y2="10" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="120" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="90" y="134" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=3Ω</text><line x1="140" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="145" y="85" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">X=4Ω</text><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="70" y="78" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Z</text></svg>
figure.caption: Impedance triangle (R–X)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A thyristor (SCR) is turned ON by:
options: A gate pulse while forward biased
questions.solution: A gate current triggers the SCR into conduction when the anode is positive; it then latches.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An induction motor develops maximum torque when the rotor resistance per phase equals:
options: The standstill rotor reactance times the slip (R₂ = sX₂)
questions.solution: Maximum torque occurs at the slip where R₂ = sX₂; the peak torque itself is independent of R₂.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An induction motor has air-gap power 30 kW and slip 0.03. The rotor copper loss is:
questions.solution: Rotor copper loss = s × Pag = 0.03 × 30 kW = 900 W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method on a balanced 3-phase load, the two readings are equal when the power factor is:
questions.solution: At unity pf the phase angle is zero and both wattmeters read equal positive power; at pf 0.5 one reads zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 4 Ω and 9 Ω are connected in series. The equivalent resistance is:
